scenario_chains.scenario_title: Reltio MDM as BDC Onboarding Accelerator

steps.summary: SAP acquires Reltio to embed AI entity resolution (golden record) into BDC
evidence: SAP announcement: acquisition aimed at integrating non-SAP data into AI platform
Reltio's entity resolution resolves fragmented enterprise records into unified golden record

steps.summary: BDC adoption gap constrains Reltio's market impact — 83% DSAG unfamiliarity binds
evidence: DSAG December 2025 research: 83% of members unfamiliar or only slightly familiar with BDC
Mid-migration billing friction creates negative customer sentiment

steps.summary: Reltio positions BDC as self-service data onboarding layer for existing SAP customers
evidence: Muhammad Alam quote: 'combining SAP and non-SAP data to deliver data context that business AI requires'
Strategic goal: extend BDC beyond SAP-data-only to heterogeneous enterprise estates

steps.summary: Gap: Reltio's technical capability alone does not close DSAG trust deficit
evidence: Trust deficit is a sales and change-management problem, not a product-feature problem
Binoy James: 'That line is going to haunt SAP's sales deck for a while'

steps.summary: Required: SAP must pair Reltio integration with a migration-simplification programme and revised pricing for mid-migration customers
evidence: Migration pricing reform is needed to convert early BDC adopters into advocates
Databricks amplifier only activates if BDC has a credible reputation among existing SAP customers

required.text: SAP must launch a migration-phase pricing reform before Reltio capabilities go GA to prevent compounding friction.
Reltio MDM onboarding must be exposed via a low-friction API layer compatible with existing customer data architectures.

warning.text: If DSAG trust deficit is not addressed within 18 months, competitive framing by Salesforce and Microsoft will frame Reltio integration as a further migration burden.

blocking.text: If mid-migration billing friction persists post-acquisition, Reltio premium pricing is blocked by customer perception of SAP as extracting from a captive base.

scenario_chains.scenario_title: Reltio Extends BDC to Data-First Enterprises via Databricks Channel

steps.summary: Databricks alliance amplifies Reltio's reach beyond core SAP customer base
evidence: BDC-Databricks partnership already established at launch (February 2025)
Reltio's cloud-native Spanner architecture is compatible with hyperscaler ecosystems

steps.summary: Databricks competing interests in open data formats may limit deep integration commitment
evidence: Databricks promotes Delta Lake and open lakehouse formats
SAP's One Domain Model is proprietary — tension may limit full integration depth

steps.summary: Reach data-first enterprises with Reltio MDM as a Databricks-native integration
evidence: Tony Baer quote: SAP acknowledged it is not the only focal point for enterprise business data
BDC's original mission: expose SAP data as products shareable with Databricks, Google BigQuery, Snowflake, Microsoft Fabric

steps.summary: Gap: No joint Reltio-Databricks integration roadmap announced at acquisition time
evidence: Acquisition announcement does not include Databricks-specific integration commitments
Databricks channel activation requires a separate go-to-market agreement

steps.summary: Required: SAP and Databricks must publicly commit to a Reltio integration milestone at Sapphire 2026 to activate this scenario
evidence: Market credibility requires a joint announcement to signal to data-first enterprises
Without Sapphire commitment, Scenario B degrades to a future optionality rather than a near-term driver

required.text: A joint SAP-Databricks announcement of Reltio integration is required at Sapphire 2026 for Scenario B to materialise.

warning.text: If SAP over-proprietary-ises the Reltio MDM layer, Databricks may deprioritise the integration in favour of competing open-MDM offerings.

scenario_chains.scenario_title: Reltio Exacerbates Migration Complexity — Adoption Stalls

steps.summary: Reltio adds integration surface area to an already-challenged BDC migration programme
evidence: Customers mid-migration already paying full cloud fees for unusable systems
Adding Reltio MDM scope may extend migration timelines and increase project risk

steps.summary: Enterprise data gravity and multi-year migration timelines bind strongly in this scenario
evidence: ERP migrations are typically 3–7 year programmes
Adding an MDM harmonisation layer is a distinct, parallel workstream with its own complexity

steps.summary: SAP's objective is BDC adoption growth — but customers see more complexity, not less
evidence: SAP's messaging positions Reltio as simplification — but customer perception is complexity addition
Consultant quote: 'That line is going to haunt SAP's sales deck'

steps.summary: Gap: SAP does not yet have a unified messaging framework that positions Reltio as a simplification tool vs. an additional layer
evidence: Public announcement is product-centric, not customer-journey-centric
No announced simplification pricing or migration support programme at acquisition

steps.summary: Blocking: Without a customer-facing simplification narrative, Scenario C becomes self-fulfilling as DSAG voices dominate the conversation
evidence: DSAG has proven ability to shape SAP enterprise customer perception
83% unfamiliarity baseline means SAP has no earned trust buffer to absorb negative signals

warning.text: If SAP does not address mid-migration billing friction within 6 months, Scenario C's stall dynamic becomes difficult to reverse.

blocking.text: Without a customer-facing simplification narrative and pricing reform, negative DSAG perception blocks BDC pipeline growth regardless of Reltio's technical merit.
